experiences.intentions: The board is empty except for the active J near the top in its spawn orientation. With an empty field, the best immediate objective is usually to place pieces quickly while preserving a flat stack and flexibility for the upcoming I, O, L, Z sequence. Candidate moves: left/right would only shift horizontally without committing; rotations are unnecessary on an empty board unless targeting a side tuck later; soft_drop advances only one row. Hard_drop efficiently places the J on the floor in its current orientation, creating a low, simple profile and progressing the game state fastest.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The active I piece is horizontal near the top, while the stack is a small J formation centered at the bottom. There is no immediate threat because the board is almost empty. The goal now is to place the I efficiently to keep the surface flat and preserve flexibility for upcoming O, L, Z, T pieces. Hard dropping the horizontal I onto the flat area just above the bottom stack will create a broad, low foundation without introducing awkward vertical wells; rotating vertical is unnecessary and slower, and moving left/right offers little improvement on this empty board.

experiences.intentions: Board is mostly empty with a settled O at the top and a J plus horizontal I stack near the bottom. The active piece appears to be the horizontal I on row 18, leaving a 3-wide gap on the left and 4-wide space on the right above the J structure. Goal is to place the I efficiently without creating holes; with an empty board and upcoming L,Z,T,S, keeping the center flexible is best. Rotations shown only allow the current horizontal orientation, so movement/drop is the key choice. A hard drop now would lock the I high on top of the J, which is poor. Moving left shifts it toward the wall for a cleaner low placement on the floor, but first action should advance positioning. Therefore choose left.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is O at the top around columns 3-4; stack below has a flat I on row 18 and a J on the right creating a small notch. O cannot rotate meaningfully, so best immediate sub-goal is lateral placement before dropping to keep the surface flatter and avoid covering the central notch awkwardly. Candidate moves: hard/soft drop now would place the O above the central stack; right would steer it over the J side and likely increase unevenness, while left moves it toward the emptier left side for a cleaner future placement with upcoming L,Z,T,S. Therefore move left this turn.

Goal is to place the I efficiently on the flat floor to avoid creating holes and preserve flexibility for upcoming L,Z,T,S. Candidate moves: dropping now would leave the I suspended on the J and create a higher messy stack; moving left would let the I reach the empty floor and make a clean 4-wide base; moving right worsens overlap with J; rotations are unnecessary on this open board. Best immediate sub-goal is shifting left toward a flat hard-drop placement.

experiences.intentions: The active T piece is high near the top in spawn orientation around columns 5-7. experiences.intentions: Active piece is an S near the top center in its spawn orientation. The stack below has a relatively flat center with some structure on the left and right; there is no immediate threat near the top, so the priority is improving placement rather than urgency. A good sub-goal is to move the S toward the right-side contour where an S can likely fit more naturally above the existing Z/L shape, while preserving the flatter middle for upcoming O,S,L,I pieces. Among candidate actions, right begins that positioning without committing; left would steer toward the T-side where the fit looks less natural, rotations are not yet necessary, and dropping now would likely place the S suboptimally high in the center. Therefore the best immediate move is to shift right.

experiences.intentions: The active O piece is at the very top centered around columns 5-6 and cannot benefit from rotation. The stack below has a relatively flat middle with some structure on the left and right; there is no immediate danger near the top, so the best sub-goal is clean placement to preserve flexibility and avoid creating holes. Candidate actions: rotate_left/right do nothing useful for O, no_op wastes time, soft_drop only advances one row, and left/right would start shifting but not finish placement. A hard drop now efficiently places the O onto the highest supporting surface in its current column, likely around the central flat area above the lower stack, maintaining a compact board and progressing toward line clears.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is an S near the top-center, currently around columns 5-7. The stack is low and there is no immediate threat, so the goal is to place the S efficiently to keep the surface smooth and set up future L/I/J/T pieces. The best nearby fit is slightly left of the current position above the central contours, and moving left preserves the flat option while avoiding committing to a weaker vertical S rotation too early. Candidate actions: hard_drop now would place it prematurely in a less considered spot; rotate_right makes a vertical S that doesn't clearly improve the board; soft_drop is unnecessary before horizontal alignment; right moves away from the better central-left fit. Therefore left is the best setup move now.

experiences.intentions: Active S piece is high near the center-top, currently horizontal over columns roughly 4-6. The stack below is low and uneven, with a broad empty field and no immediate danger. Goal is to place the S efficiently to keep the surface flat and avoid creating isolated overhangs. Since there is plenty of vertical space, the strongest move now is to commit the piece downward rather than spend turns shifting; dropping it in its current central position should land it on the existing mid-right structure and preserve access for upcoming L, I, J, T pieces. Left/right or rotation are possible, but there is no urgent tactical gain visible from a single-step adjustment compared with securing placement immediately.

experiences.intentions: Active L is high and unobstructed. The stack is concentrated low with a relatively flat central-right surface and no immediate top-out threat. Goal now is efficient placement while preserving flexibility for upcoming I/J/T/Z pieces. Candidate moves: hard_drop would commit immediately in current orientation near center-left, but that may be premature. Rotating could create better tuck options later, but since the piece is still high, first improving horizontal alignment is stronger. Moving right shifts the L toward the open center/right lanes above the current stack, giving more landing choices next turn; left moves away from the main contour. Soft drop/no_op provide less strategic gain than horizontal positioning now.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is an L near the top with lots of vertical space. The stack is low but somewhat uneven; the left-center around columns 2-4 has a shallow cavity above existing T/I/J pieces, while the right side is already busier with S/Z/O/L blocks. Goal is to place the L to improve surface flatness and avoid creating overhangs. Immediate candidates: hard_drop in current orientation would land the L flat across columns 2-4 with its foot at column 4, fitting the left-center contour well; moving right would crowd the messier right side, and rotating first is unnecessary because current orientation already looks placeable. So the best single action now is to commit the good placement quickly with a hard drop.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is an I tetromino currently vertical near column 5 at the top. Surface below has a clear 4-wide horizontal gap on row 17 at columns 3-6, ideal for a horizontal I placement. Goal is to set up/complete a line efficiently without creating holes. Candidate actions: rotate_right would make the I horizontal but still high; left/right would only shift while vertical. hard_drop now would place a bad vertical stack. soft_drop advances downward but misses the key orientation change. Best immediate sub-goal is to rotate to horizontal first so it can then be moved/dropped into the 4-wide slot.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is an I horizontally near the top with plenty of space; rotating vertically would create a good chance to slot it into the open center-right well above the stack. Immediate threats are low since the piece is high and the board top is empty. Goal is to place the I efficiently for future line clears while preserving flexibility for upcoming J,T,Z,O. Among actions, rotate_right changes the I to vertical immediately, which is the key setup before lateral movement/drop; left/right now without rotating is less purposeful, and dropping horizontally would waste the long bar placement opportunity.

experiences.intentions: The active I piece is vertical in column 4 near the top, with ample empty space below until it would contact the existing stack around row 8. The board surface has some unevenness, but there is a clear opportunity: placing the I horizontally across row 7 (0-indexed from top in the render) would fill columns 3-6 on an otherwise empty row and create a flatter transition above the central stack. Rotating now is safe because there is open space around the piece. Alternatives: hard_drop immediately as vertical would create a tall central column and is less useful; moving left/right first is unnecessary if the horizontal placement at current x is already good; soft_drop delays the beneficial orientation change. Best sub-goal is to convert the I to horizontal now to prepare for a clean drop next.

experiences.intentions: The active piece is a vertical I high in the stack around column 5. The board surface has several uneven regions, but there is a clear deep vertical well at column 4 above the J block near the bottom, and moving the I piece left while keeping it vertical is a strong setup because it can later drop cleanly into that 4-cell gap for an efficient line clear. Rotating horizontal now would place it awkwardly across upper holes and likely block future placements. Immediate goal is to align the I with the open well; among candidate actions, left moves directly toward that target, right moves away, drops commit too early, and rotation is inferior. So the best action now is to shift left.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active J is at the top with plenty of empty space below; the stack is low and there is no immediate threat. The board’s upper area is empty, so the best short-term goal is to start moving the J toward a useful landing zone rather than locking it immediately in a poor central spot. A left move begins shifting the piece toward the left-side contours while preserving flexibility for later rotations/drops; no_op wastes time, right moves away from more open fitting options, rotations are premature, and dropping now would commit too early.

Surface has a notable vertical well around column 4 above the lower stack; placing the J flat now won’t lock, so best immediate sub-goal is horizontal alignment toward a future tuck/stack without creating overhangs. Candidate actions: left moves the J toward the left side but away from the central gap; right shifts it toward the central columns where a J can later slot more cleanly. Rotations change shape early in open space but are not yet necessary. Dropping now would commit too early from the spawn height. So move right.

experiences.intentions: Active piece appears to be a vertical I in column 5 with only its current vertical orientation valid; rotating is blocked. Surface near center-right is uneven, and dropping now is safe. Goal is to place the I efficiently without wasting time; moving laterally from this height risks misalignment while immediate descent progresses toward a stable placement. Candidate actions: rotate_left/right are invalid, left/right may still be possible but there is no clear better lane from the given stack, hard_drop commits immediately on a rough surface, while soft_drop advances the piece downward and preserves flexibility next turn.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active T is near the top with plenty of vertical space; the stack below is uneven but not in immediate danger. There is a useful notch around the upper-middle columns, and preserving flexibility is better than dropping immediately. Rotating to orientation 1 puts the T in a vertical shape that can be steered into columns/gaps on subsequent moves; hard/soft drop now would commit too early, and lateral movement before choosing orientation is less informative. Best sub-goal is to orient the T for cleaner placement options first.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active T is high near the top with open space below. The stack is uneven but not in immediate danger, and there is no need to lock this piece yet. A useful sub-goal is to steer the T toward the center-left where future placement options around the surface near rows 15-19 may be better than the current right-side column. Candidate actions: hard_drop commits too early from a poor high position; soft_drop reduces flexibility; rotations are not clearly improving the shape now. Moving left starts repositioning the T toward more open landing lanes and keeps all options available.

Surface below has a notable cavity/vertical gap around columns 3-4 and a flat-ish center-right; immediate threats are low since stack is not near the top. Goal is to place T to improve the mid-left topology and preserve future placements from upcoming Z,O,J. From the shown orientations, the current piece is in rotation 1; rotating right to rotation 2 creates a flat T-top shape that is generally versatile for slotting into surface notches. Compared with moving or dropping immediately, taking the rotation now improves future landing options while still safe at this height.

experiences.intentions: The active T piece is high and currently around columns 2-4 above a fairly uneven midgame stack. There is no immediate top-out threat, so the priority is to improve placement flexibility before committing. The lower board has a left-center T-shaped cavity around rows 15-16, but the piece is still far above it, and dropping now in the current orientation would strand it on upper surface bumps. Among candidates, rotating to orientation 1 creates a vertical T with the stem down, which is the most versatile for threading into future gaps as it descends. Horizontal movement can be done after rotation. Hard or soft drop now is premature; no_op wastes a chance to orient. So the best sub-goal is to set the piece into its most useful descent orientation now.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Active T is near the top with space around it; the surface below has a central notch/uneven stack and no immediate danger at spawn height. Goal is to place the T efficiently while preserving flexibility for upcoming Z,O,J. Rotating right from the current shown orientation to rotation 0 creates a flatter T shape across row 6, improving horizontal placement options before committing downward. Other actions either move without improving fit, drop too early, or rotate to less useful vertical states.

experiences.intentions: The active piece is an I in vertical orientation near the upper-right center. Rotations at the current position all collide, so rotation is not available now. The stack below has uneven surfaces with no immediate one-column well directly under this I for a strong hard drop, and dropping now would likely commit it high on the right. My goal is to improve placement flexibility and set up future pieces (Z,O,J,Z) while avoiding a bad early lock. Moving left shifts the I toward the more open center-left lanes and may open future vertical or horizontal placements on subsequent turns. Right appears more constrained by existing blocks; dropping now is premature; no_op wastes a turn.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is a Z at the top. Current spawn orientation sits with top row at columns 4-5 and second row at 5-6. Rotating right would shift it into a vertical Z shape that can start fitting narrower gaps; immediate hard/soft drop now would land on the central stack without a clear setup, while lateral moves are less urgent than getting the useful orientation first. Goal is to place the Z to improve surface fit and avoid creating overhangs; sub-goal is orienting it for better future placement before descent. Candidate actions: left/right only translate current flat Z; soft/hard drop commits too early; no_op wastes a turn. Best is rotate_right.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is a J near the top with room to maneuver; the stack is uneven with a notable vertical channel around columns 4-5. Immediate threats are low because the piece is high and board top is open. Goal is to place the J to improve surface and avoid sealing useful wells; a good sub-goal is to orient it vertically so it can move into the central gap on subsequent turns. Candidate actions: hard_drop/soft_drop would commit too early from the spawn area; left/right without rotation is less flexible because the current horizontal J shape is bulky. Rotating right changes it to a compact vertical J orientation that better fits the center-left channel and preserves future placement options.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.
